data.description:
## Context

The plan-review claim gate (verify.require_plan_review_for_claim) binds the attestation to the exact code HEAD sha and treats a HEAD mismatch as 'stale', blocking the claim. Because the binding is to the WHOLE commit (not the code the review was actually grounded against), ANY subsequent commit — even one in a completely unrelated file — invalidates a freshly-earned, still-correct attestation.

This is the over-invalidation / false-positive sibling of this epic's under-invalidation concern: 4fcd is about CODE drift NOT being detected (the attestation only binds ticket-material, so relevant code can drift undetected). This bug is the inverse: IRRELEVANT code changes DO invalidate, adding real friction. Both share the root cause — the code-binding is too coarse (whole-HEAD) rather than scoped to the review's resolved edit-set / cited files. The mechanism in this epic's AC1 ('detects material drift in the code ... e.g. the resolved edit-set / cited files') would fix both facets at once.

## Reproduction Steps
1. On a feature branch, run `rebar review-plan <ticketA>` at HEAD=X -> earns a signed attestation bound to X.
2. Commit unrelated work (e.g. another ticket's code, or even a docs typo) -> HEAD advances to Y.
3. Run `rebar claim <ticketA> --assignee me`.

## Expected
The claim succeeds: the attestation is still valid because none of the code ticketA's plan was grounded against changed.

## Actual
The claim is BLOCKED: `Error: cannot claim <ticketA>: attestation is stale (signed at X, HEAD is Y).` The only recovery is to re-run the heavy LLM `review-plan` (minutes + billable) purely because HEAD moved, even though nothing relevant drifted. Observed repeatedly while implementing epic ring-gun-jot: committing sweep N's code staled the already-earned attestation for sweep N+1, forcing a re-review.

## Impact / friction
- Forces serialization (review-plan -> claim must straddle zero intervening commits) and defeats pre-earning attestations in parallel.
- Re-review is slow (~3-5 min) and billable, incurred for no semantic reason.
- Especially painful on multi-ticket branches and shared-checkout test runs that move HEAD.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Claim freshness is scoped to the code the review was grounded against (resolved edit-set / cited files) rather than the whole HEAD sha, so an unrelated commit does NOT stale the attestation.
- [ ] A relevant change (to the cited/edit-set files) STILL invalidates (no regression of this epic's under-invalidation goal).
- [ ] Tested: review at X, commit an unrelated file, claim succeeds; review at X, commit a cited file, claim is blocked.
- [ ] Recovery guidance / friction is reduced (no full re-review required for an unrelated commit).
data.title: Stale-attestation claim block: ANY new commit (even unrelated) invalidates a fresh plan-review attestation
